title: The Tim Ferriss Show Transcripts: Mark Zuckerberg on Long-Term Strategy, Business and Parenting Principles, Personal Energy Management, Building the Metaverse, Seeking Awe, the Role of Religion, Solving Deep Technical Challenges (e.g., AR), and More (#582) - The Blog of Author Tim Ferriss
excerpts:
Section Title: The Tim Ferriss Show Transcripts: Mark Zuckerberg on Long-Term Strategy, Business and Parenting P...
Content:
1 comment
Topics: [The Tim Ferriss Show Transcripts](https://tim.blog/category/the-tim-ferriss-show-transcripts/)
Please enjoy this transcript of [my interview with Mark Zuckerberg](https://tim.blog/2022/03/24/mark-zuckerberg/) ( [FB](http://www.facebook.com/zuck) / [IG](http://www.instagram.com/zuck) ), the founder, chairman, and CEO of [Meta](https://meta.com) , which he originally founded as Facebook in 2004. Mark is responsible for setting the overall direction and product strategy for the company. In October 2021, Facebook rebranded to Meta to reflect all of its products and services across its family of apps and a focus on developing social experiences for the metaverse—moving beyond 2D screens toward immersive experiences like augmented and virtual reality to help build the next evolution in social technology.

title: Mark Zuckerberg is creating an… - The Claire Byrne Show - Apple Podcasts
excerpts:
Section Title: Mark Zuckerberg is creating an AI version of himself
Content:
[The Claire Byrne Show](https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/the-claire-byrne-show/id1496321249)
Play
Mark Zuckerberg is creating an AI version of himself so that staff can talk to him. The AI clone of Zuckerberg, Meta’s founder and chief executive, is being trained on his mannerisms and tone as well as his public statements and thoughts on company strategy.  Why, why and how? Newstalk's Technology Correspondent Jess Kelly explains all.

title: Extra: Mark Zuckerberg Full Interview - Freakonomics
excerpts:
Section Title: Extra: Mark Zuckerberg Full Interview > Episode Transcript
Content:
What follows is **Stephen Dubner** ’s conversation with Facebook founder and C.E.O. [Mark Zuckerberg,](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mark_Zuckerberg) recorded for the *Freakonomics Radio* series “ [The Secret Life of a C.E.O.](http://www.freakonomics.com/ceos) ” It was recorded last summer, long before we learned that 50 million Facebook users’ data had been weaponized by political operatives. Facebook has been the subject of intense scrutiny for years now; that’s what happens when you’ve gone from a college dorm startup to a social network with some 2 billion global users. We spoke with Zuckerberg in Chicago in a trailer, outside an event space where he’d just addressed a few hundred very enthusiastic people who serve as group administrators for Facebook user groups. He introduced new software tools that would help them manage their groups.

title: Zuckerberg Interview on Fact Checking - Rev
excerpts:
Section Title: Mark Zuckerberg Interview Transcript on Social Media Fact Checking Politics & Remote Work
Content:
And I think something like remote work can help on that. Mark Zuckerberg: ( [02:59](https://www.rev.com/transcript-editor/shared/E8i0rEmhIYoDMzm0FocWIggycpW5KBaA0RDGK1hr4al_lGjRWOaZFWMqGZFS4qqfMuzhBQBjPyjhDJBongYDh2tlBPw?loadFrom=PastedDeeplink&ts=179.89) ) There's environmental positive aspects. People are going to spend a lot less time commuting and more time just teleporting in, either over video chat or eventually things like virtual reality. And for us, I mean, our company, so much of what we do is just building products that help people feel connected and present together no matter where they are. So whether that's the main kind of feed products that we offer or things like video chat workplace for enterprises, our hardware with portal, or the longer term bets are on virtual and augmented reality that are really about helping you feel present.

title: Mark Zuckerberg: The Recode interview - Vox
excerpts:
Section Title: Zuckerberg: The Recode interview
Content:
You can listen to our entire conversation right now in the audio player below. If you prefer to listen on your phone, **Recode Decode** is available wherever you listen to podcasts — including [Apple Podcasts](https://itunes.apple.com/us/podcast/recode-decode-hosted-by-kara-swisher/id1011668648?mt=2) , [Spotify](https://open.spotify.com/show/47jQcyRcrM1EoV0sU39N9F) , [Pocket Casts](http://pca.st/recode) and [Overcast.](https://overcast.fm/itunes1011668648/recode-decode-hosted-by-kara-swisher)
What follows is a condensed, lightly edited version of the conversation. You can read a [full transcript of the interview here.](https://www.recode.net/2018/7/18/17575158/mark-zuckerberg-facebook-interview-full-transcript-kara-swisher)
**Kara Swisher:** **I’m gonna start off with the news of the day. You saw the Putin/Trump press conference, essentially.**
I saw the news about it.
